pr.title: Update README with required tools for building
pr.author: vinceau

summary: Documentation-only PR that clarified the README build section after the author ran into issues building the DOL. It renamed the build section to "Building", added a required tools subsection for devkitPro, Python3, and gcc, clarified that baserom.dol belongs in the base working directory rather than the build folder, and simplified the final instruction to run make.

key_files.role: Updated build documentation and required tool list

what_changed: Renamed the README section from "Build Instructions" to "Building".
Added a "Required tools" subsection listing devkitPro, Python3, and gcc.
Documented MSYS pacman install commands for Python3 and gcc: `pacman -S msys/python3` and `pacman -S gcc`.
Added an "Instructions" subsection under the build section.
Changed the baserom placement instruction from the build folder to the base working directory, still named `baserom.dol`.
Changed the final build step from "Build tools and `make`." to "Run the `make` command".
Left the existing MWCC Build 158 PowerPC / CodeWarrior 1.1 compiler acquisition note intact.
smart_moves: Separated prerequisites from procedural build steps, making the README easier for new contributors to scan.
Documented concrete package-manager commands for required host tools instead of only naming the tools.
Corrected the baserom.dol location to the base working directory, reducing a likely setup failure for first-time builders.
Kept the licensing-sensitive MWCC note explicit: the project does not provide a license or crack and users must obtain access themselves.
decomp_lessons: For decomp projects, build documentation should distinguish required system tools, proprietary compiler setup, baserom placement, and the actual make invocation.
README setup instructions should specify exact paths for legally obtained assets such as `baserom.dol`; vague placement like "build folder" can cause avoidable build errors.
When a project depends on proprietary compilers, documentation should explain expected executable names and install/extract locations while avoiding distribution or licensing promises.
Small onboarding fixes can matter because early build failures block contributors before they can work on matching or decompilation tasks.
naming_conventions: Uses `baserom.dol` as the expected name for the clean Melee 1.2 DOL.
Documents MWCC compiler location under `tools/mwcc_compiler/1.1/` or extracted into `tools/mwcc_compiler/`.
Names the MWCC executables as `mwcceppc.exe`, `mwasmeppc.exe`, and `mwldeppc.exe`.
Uses README headings `Building`, `Required tools`, and `Instructions` for build setup structure.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states the motivation: the author ran into issues building the DOL and added clarification to the README.
follow_up_queries: Find later README or build-system PRs that changed baserom.dol placement or setup instructions.
Search for PRs documenting devkitPro, MSYS, pacman, Python3, or gcc dependencies.
Look for later changes around `tools/mwcc_compiler` path conventions and MWCC executable names.
Identify whether later onboarding docs replaced the private Discord / GC_COMPILERS.zip compiler acquisition note.
